sections: Frozen full-file baseline at git:214a5b8eb182fce74478df49d4f55d226d65fdf5 with SHA-256 c7f61db209a81ba20690f365b4069dd01f11e395335bfa10d2ce21143cc2985d
Terminal Planning Review 3 subject manifest and review contract
Continuation Review 1 exact subject manifest and immutable review contract

repo_truth: Planning/control-only Continuation Review 2 candidate on feat/handbook-contract-membrane at preserved checkpoint HEAD 754970ef141c4e9efcce8ca6e76c683dbb248a11. The six manifest files are the only bytes under review. Continuation Review 1 returned two Required findings: named-section aggregate fanout triggered the topology-redesign stop, and broad/overlapping operation routes were incomplete. The one permitted intervening remediation rejects flat named-section union routing and replaces it with trigger-indexed routing: headings are locators only; exact R*/O* triggers activate targets; unions of six or more leaves are broad-only. The independently derived audit now covers 11 spans, 48 H2, 22 H3, 84 positional occurrences, 98 exact routes R01-R98, 50 per-operation fixtures O01-O50 (38 routed, 12 explicit non-routes), 11 named non-route groups N01-N11, and all 70 heading aggregates. Exactly three heading aggregates reach seven leaves and are broad-only; every exact route targets at most three siblings. R33-era broad operation families are split per operation, omitted owner anchors are present, and the Dock request overlap is split into exact field triggers. Unchanged 05/07/08, historical evidence, and both continuation dispatches are contextual authority only, not subject bytes. HCM-0.9 execution is not authorized in this session.
allowed_scope: Replay the exact six-file subject manifest against the live repository and review those bytes read-only.

Independently verify the all-eleven audit, 98 exact routes, 50 operation fixtures, 11 non-route groups, positional inventory, target anchors, 70 heading aggregates, and trigger-indexed routing topology.
Re-evaluate both Continuation Review 1 Required findings against live authority, including every ordinary operation and the Dock request/result overlap repair.
Review the subject-manifest versus unchanged authority-reference rule everywhere represented in the six-file subject.
Run read-only baseline, Markdown, audit, scope, archive, handoff, Git, and GitNexus checks as useful.
non_goals: No edits, commits, new dispatches, handoff records, ledger writes, or user task hops by the reviewer.
No decomposition or edit of 05-contracts-schemas-and-gates.md; no HCM-0.9 execution.
No Rust, Cargo, runtime, CLI, Tauri, Substrate, SDK/public API, HCM-0.5, topology redesign without audit proof, or unrelated planning improvement.
Do not rely on Planning Review 3 conclusions or the parent validation summary; independently judge the exact current subject and live authority.
tasks: Replay the sorted path/SHA-256 manifest and confirm it contains only and all six reviewed files; unchanged contextual authority must remain outside it.
Verify baseline identity, all eleven spans, all 48 H2/22 H3 headings, and all 84 positional classifications from frozen bytes.

Verify R01-R98 semantically and mechanically: every exact trigger is justified and non-overlapping, every target is complete and resolves, source leaves 1/5/11 are covered, and no omitted/unjustified route remains.

Verify O01-O50 against every frozen ordinary operation, especially profile.resolve, schema.read, artifact.instance.list, artifact.validate, resolution.stack.read, projection.create, snapshot.project, record.list/read, and snapshot.resolve_applicable; prove the 12 non-routes are justified.
Verify N01-N11 and the split Dock Resolution/canonical-artifact/projection-artifact/Snapshot triggers reject the prior R48/R49 overlap.

Independently derive aggregate fanout for every H2/H3. Confirm flat heading-union routing is rejected, the three seven-leaf heading unions are broad-only, headings alone activate no routes, multi-trigger unions are computed before loading, and no exact normal route exceeds source plus three siblings.
Check every planning representation requires omitted, broken, overlapping, unjustified, missing-operation, unclassified, implicit-heading-activation, and stale-fanout negative proof.
Check subject manifests contain only reviewed bytes, changed routing/proof files are manifested when reviewed, and unchanged contextual authority remains only in authority_refs/contracts_and_gates.
Return findings first ordered Critical, Required, Optional, Nit with exact file/line evidence and smallest remediation. Return CLEAN only when no actionable finding remains.
contracts_and_gates: Human-authorized bounded continuation: preserve terminal Review 3 evidence; remediate only HCM-0.9-PR3-001/002 and validated continuation findings; Continuation Review 2 is terminal and no third continuation review is allowed
HCM-0.4 frozen 05 baseline at git:214a5b8eb182fce74478df49d4f55d226d65fdf5 and SHA-256 c7f61db209a81ba20690f365b4069dd01f11e395335bfa10d2ce21143cc2985d
Trigger-indexed routing topology: headings are locators; six-or-more-leaf unions are broad-only; normal exact triggers target at most three siblings
Exact dependency completeness: R01-R98, O01-O50, N01-N11, all 70 heading aggregates, no omitted/broken/overlapping/unjustified/unclassified route
Subject manifests contain reviewed bytes only; unchanged contextual authority is referenced but not manifested by default
Continuation Review 2 of maximum two; if not CLEAN, do not authorize execution and create a partial human-input handoff
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the complete manifest subject, frozen baseline, or required read-only validation is unavailable.
Return Critical or Required rather than authorizing decomposition, execution, Rust/runtime work, HCM-0.5, unrelated improvement, historical rewrite, or unjustified topology.
Do not modify repository state or write orchestration artifacts.
